1. Stop the engines completely.
2. Open the rear platform hatch.
3. Remove the inspection cover shown below. This cover gives you access to the shut-off valve area.

<PIC>

1 Inspection cover

4. To turn the water supply ON, turn the shut-off valve 90 degrees clockwise.

<PIC>

1 Shut-off valve

5. To turn the water supply OFF, turn the shut-off valve 90 degrees counterclockwise.
6. Reinstall the inspection cover securely.
7. Close the rear platform hatch and make sure it is fully latched.

Tip: If the water supply will not be used for an extended period, leave the shut-off valve in the OFF/closed position.,['Manual09_179', 'Manual09_180']

The removal procedure depends on whether your boat is the 210FSH SPORT or the 210FSH DELUXE.

For the 210FSH SPORT:

1. Open the flap on the starboard side of the bimini top. This gives you access to the securing strap and ratchet area.

<PIC>

2. Loosen the ratchet first, then loosen the strap that secures the bimini top. Make sure the strap is fully slack before pulling on the top.

<PIC>

3. Slowly pull the bimini top toward the bow and remove it from the T-top frame. Use a controlled motion so the fabric and frame do not bind or snag.

For the 210FSH DELUXE:

1. First place the bimini top in the fully collapsed position. This means the top should be folded down and resting on the rear deck before you remove it from the boat.

2. Once the bimini top is fully collapsed, remove the main pole mounting pins that secure the bimini top to the main pole mounting brackets. Support the bimini top while removing the pins so it does not shift unexpectedly.

<PIC>

3. Lift the bimini top away from the mounting brackets and remove it from the boat.

Tip: Keep all mounting pins and hardware together after removal so they are ready for reinstallation.,['Manual09_183', 'Manual09_184', 'Manual09_193']

If you want to use the canopy, the setup depends on whether your boat is a 210FSH SPORT or a 210FSH DELUXE.

For the 210FSH SPORT, the T-top is equipped with a removable bimini top. Once installed, it can be used as a sunshade or rain cover over the leaning post area.

<PIC>

To install the bimini top on the 210FSH SPORT, reverse the removal procedure:

1. Position the bimini top back onto the T-top frame. During removal, the top is pulled slowly toward the bow; for installation, guide it back into place in the opposite direction so it sits correctly on the T-top.

2. Tighten the strap that secures the bimini top, then tighten the ratchet so the top is held firmly in place.

<PIC>

3. Close the flap on the starboard side of the bimini top to finish securing the canopy.

<PIC>

For the 210FSH DELUXE, set up the bimini top as follows:

1. Raise the bimini top from its stored position.

<PIC>

2. Secure each forward support pole to its forward support pole mounting bracket using the lock pin. Make sure the lock pins are fully inserted so the support poles are locked in place.

<PIC>

3. Remove the storage cover, unfold the bimini top, and pull the canopy toward the stern.

<PIC>

4. Push each center pole upward, then install the lock pin to secure the center pole and hold the bimini top in the extended position.

<PIC>

Important: Do not exceed 72 km/h (45 mph) when the bimini top is in the up position.,['Manual09_182', 'Manual09_184', 'Manual09_183', 'Manual09_185', 'Manual09_186', 'Manual09_187', 'Manual09_188']

To store the bimini top in the upright position, please follow these steps:

NOTICE: Do not trailer the boat with the bimini top in the fully extended position or in the upright storage position. If you will be trailering the boat, place the bimini top in the fully collapsed position to help prevent damage.

1. Remove the lock pins from the center poles, then push the center poles down. The image below shows the center pole being lowered and the lock pin being pulled out.

<PIC>

2. Pull the bimini top toward the bow of the boat until it moves into the upright storage position. The arrow in the image shows the correct direction to pull the top.

<PIC>

3. Install the storage cover over the bimini top to secure and protect it while it is stored upright.,['Manual09_189', 'Manual09_190']

Yes. If you need to check the engine oil level while you are on the water, first move to a safe location and securely moor the boat so it will not drift. Keep the boat as level as possible, stop the engines before opening the engine hood, and use caution because the engine oil can be extremely hot immediately after operation.

Follow these steps for each engine:

1. With the engine stopped, make sure the boat is level on the water.
2. Look in all directions to confirm it is safe, then start the engine.
3. Run the engine at idle speed for at least 6 minutes. If the ambient temperature is 20°C / 68°F or lower, idle it for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Stop the engine.
5. Open the engine hood.
6. Loosen and remove the oil tank filler cap. The dipstick is attached to this cap. Wipe the dipstick clean before measuring.

<PIC>

1 Oil tank filler cap/Dipstick

7. Screw the oil tank filler cap fully back into the filler hole until it stops, then remove it again. Check that the oil level is between the minimum and maximum marks on the dipstick.

<PIC>

1 Dipstick  2 Maximum level mark  3 Minimum level mark

8. If the oil level is below the minimum mark, slowly add the recommended 4-stroke engine oil, then recheck the level. Repeat the wipe, install, remove, and read process until the oil is within the correct range.
9. If the oil level is significantly above the maximum mark, do not continue operating normally; have the boat checked by a Boat Dealer.
10. Once the level is correct, securely install the oil tank filler cap and turn it until it stops.
11. Repeat the same oil level check on the other engine.
12. Close the engine hood securely.

Important safety notes:

- Engine oil may be very hot right after the engines are turned off. Avoid skin contact and keep oil off your clothing to prevent burns.
- Do not run the engine with too much or too little oil, as this can damage the engine.
- Keep water, dirt, and debris from entering the oil tank filler hole while the cap is removed.
- If a low oil pressure warning appears while underway, reduce engine speed, return to shore or move to a safe location, and check the engine oil level as described above.,['Manual09_196', 'Manual09_197']

Your boat has a bilge drainage system that directs water entering from the storage compartments into the bilge area under the engine compartment. The bilge pump is used to remove this water while the boat is on the water.

To operate it manually, turn on the bilge pump switch at the helm. Once the switch is turned on, the bilge pump will start operating.

<PIC>

The pump can also work automatically. Even if the bilge pump switch is not turned on, the pump will detect excessive water in the bilge and automatically discharge most of it through the bilge pump outlet on the side of the boat. This automatic function can still operate even when the battery switches are in the OFF position.

<PIC>

While the bilge pump is running, the bilge pump indicator light will illuminate, so you can confirm that the pump is operating.

<PIC>

If the bilge pump starts automatically, it will continue running until most of the bilge water has been drained.

Important: Do not run the engines at full throttle while bilge water remains in the engine compartment. The water can splash into the engines and may cause severe engine damage. Also make sure all drain plugs are securely tightened before operating the boat, as loose plugs can allow water to enter the boat.,['Manual09_201', 'Manual09_202', 'Manual09_203']

The navigation and anchor lights switch check should not be performed while sailing. It is a pre-launch check, so it should be completed while the boat is on land before you get underway.

Use the following procedure:

1. If you have a 210FSH DELUXE, install the removable anchor light before checking the switch.

2. Locate the navigation and anchor lights switch on the helm switch panel. It is the NAV/ANC switch.

<PIC>

3. Push the upper side of the switch to the NAV position. Confirm that both the bow light and the anchor light turn on. This is the setting used for night running or low-visibility operation.

<PIC>

For the 210FSH DELUXE, confirm the pole-mounted anchor light at the stern is illuminated.

<PIC>

For the 210FSH SPORT, confirm the anchor light on the T-top is illuminated.

<PIC>

4. Push the lower side of the switch to the ANC position. Confirm that only the anchor light turns on. This is the setting used when anchored at night.

5. Move the switch to the middle position. Confirm that both the bow light and anchor light are off.

6. If you have a 210FSH DELUXE, remove and store the anchor light after the check is complete.

If you are already sailing and need the lights for operation, do not treat this as the pre-launch check. Simply use the correct switch position: NAV for both bow and anchor lights while running at night or in reduced visibility, and ANC for the anchor light only when anchored in the dark.,['Manual09_223', 'Manual09_224', 'Manual09_225', 'Manual09_226']

To start the boat’s engines, please follow these steps in order:

1. Before starting, make sure the boat is already launched and is in suitable water. The water should be free of weeds, debris, sand, or other objects that could be drawn into the jet intakes, and it must be at least 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat.

<PIC>

2. Turn the battery switch to the ON position. For normal operation, the START and HOUSE battery switches should be ON, and the EMERG PARALLEL switch should remain OFF unless you are using it for an emergency start.

3. Push the blower switch to run the blowers and ventilate the engine compartment for at least 4 minutes before starting. Before starting, also check the engine compartment for gasoline odor, gasoline vapors, or loose electrical connections. If you smell gasoline or notice a loose connection, do not start the engines.

<PIC>

4. Attach the engine shut-off cord, also called the lanyard, securely to your PFD. Then install the clip onto the engine shut-off switch by pushing the clip groove over the nut beneath the knob. Make sure the cord is not wrapped around the steering wheel and is not tangled with any controls.

<PIC>

5. Put both remote control levers in the neutral position. The starter motors will not operate unless the levers are in neutral.

6. Turn the main switch keys to the START position. Release each key as soon as the engine starts; the key will return to the ON position automatically. If an engine does not start after 5 seconds of cranking, release the key and wait at least 15 seconds before trying again.

<PIC>

Important: Do not turn a main switch key to START while the engine is already running, as this can damage the starter mechanism. Also, do not crank the starter continuously for more than 5 seconds, because it can quickly discharge the battery and may damage the starter motor.

After the engines start, remember that this boat’s engines are directly connected to the drive units, so some thrust may be produced immediately even when the levers are in neutral. Keep the area around the boat clear and apply only the throttle needed to keep the engines running smoothly.,['Manual09_233', 'Manual09_235', 'Manual09_236', 'Manual09_237']

To turn the boat while underway, turn the steering wheel in the direction you want the boat to go: turn left to go left, and turn right to go right. On this boat, steering response comes from jet thrust, so the amount of throttle you apply is just as important as the steering wheel position.

The jet pumps draw in water and expel it through the jet thrust nozzles. When you turn the steering wheel, the nozzles change angle, directing the thrust and changing the boat’s direction. Higher engine speed creates more thrust, which makes the boat turn more strongly.

More throttle produces higher jet thrust, so the boat will turn more sharply.
<PIC>

Less throttle produces lower jet thrust, so the boat will turn more gradually.
<PIC>

If you pull the remote control levers back to idle or neutral, only minimum thrust is produced. If you are above trolling speed, your steering ability will quickly decrease. You may have a brief amount of steering response right after reducing throttle, but as the engines slow, the boat will no longer respond effectively to steering wheel input until throttle is applied again or the boat slows to trolling speed.
<PIC>

If the boat is moving with the remote control levers in neutral, or if the engines are stopped and there is no thrust, the boat will continue straight even if the steering wheel is turned. In short: you need throttle to steer.
<PIC>

For a left turn, rotate the steering wheel counterclockwise.
<PIC>

For a right turn, rotate the steering wheel clockwise.
<PIC>

A few important safety points: make sure passengers are seated and holding on before turning, especially before sharper turns. Do not pull the remote control levers back to idle when trying to steer away from an object, because reducing throttle reduces the thrust needed for steering. Also remember that boats steer from the stern, so the stern swings outward in the opposite direction of the turn; allow extra clearance around docks, swimmers, and other obstacles.,['Manual09_239', 'Manual09_240', 'Manual09_241', 'Manual09_242', 'Manual09_243', 'Manual09_244']

When crossing wakes or swells, the goal is to reduce the impact on both passengers and the boat while maintaining control. Adjust your speed first, then choose a suitable crossing angle. In most situations, slowing down and “quartering” the wake or swell, meaning crossing it at an angle rather than straight on, will make the ride smoother and reduce the jolt.

<PIC>

As shown above, the crossing angle makes a noticeable difference. A 90-degree approach may produce a sharper impact, while an angled approach such as about 45 degrees usually helps the boat ride over the wake more comfortably. However, when crossing at a smaller angle, such as around 10 degrees, the wake or swell can push against the hull more strongly and the boat may try to steer away from it. Be ready to correct with the steering wheel and keep your balance as needed.

Also keep in mind that crossing several wakes or swells in a row is usually less smooth than crossing a single wake. Reduce speed early, keep passengers seated and holding on, and avoid aggressive maneuvers or jumping wakes or waves.

<PIC>

The shape of the water also affects the ride. Small swells are usually easier to cross than larger swells or wakes. A sharp wake, like the steep waves shown in the top part of the image, will usually create a stronger jolt. A broad swell, like the rounded waves shown in the lower part, is generally smoother to pass over.

In short: slow down, cross at a controlled angle, avoid jumping the wake, and be prepared to steer and balance as the boat reacts to the water conditions.,['Manual09_249', 'Manual09_250']

To load the boat back onto the trailer after your cruise, follow these steps carefully:

1. Disconnect the trailer lights from the towing vehicle before backing the trailer into the water.

2. Back the trailer down the ramp as close to a 90-degree angle to the shoreline as possible. If available, have another person stand safely to the side of the trailer to observe and guide you. Stop backing when the tops of the trailer fenders are about 3 inches above the waterline.

3. Approach the trailer at the slowest idle speed and guide the boat onto the trailer support rails. Use throttle only when needed for steering control.

WARNING: Do not use excessive throttle while loading. Too much throttle can cause the boat to jump over the front of the trailer, which may seriously injure the operator or nearby bystanders.

4. Confirm that the boat is centered on the support rails and pointed straight toward the bow stop, also called the bumper board. Ease the boat forward slowly until the bow rests against the bow stop.

NOTICE: The winch line is not intended to pull the boat onto the trailer. The boat should already be properly positioned before the winch line is tightened.

5. Attach the winch line to the bow eye and tighten it securely. Also use the safety chain together with the winch line to secure the bow during trailering.

<PIC>

1 Bow eye

6. Pull the trailer up the ramp and move out of the launch area so other boaters can use the ramp. Once clear, attach the bow and stern tie-downs, then reconnect the trailer lights.

<PIC>

1 Stern eye

7. After the boat is loaded and secured, complete the post-operation checks before leaving or storing the boat.,['Manual09_255', 'Manual09_256']

Flushing the cooling system helps prevent salt, sand, dirt, and other debris from clogging the cooling passages. Perform this procedure after operating the boat, especially after use in salt water or dirty/sandy water.

Important safety notes before you begin:
- Flush the system only in an open, well-ventilated area.
- Make sure the area around the boat is clear before starting the engine.
- Start the engine before turning on the water.
- Turn off the water before stopping the engine.
- Do not leave water running when the engine is not running, as water may flow back through the muffler into the crankcase and cause severe engine damage.

1. Connect the garden hose adapter to a garden hose.

<PIC>

In the image: 1 = Garden hose adapter, 2 = Garden hose.

2. Remove the flush hose connector cap. Then insert the garden hose adapter into the flush hose connector by pushing it in and twisting it until it is securely connected.

<PIC>

In the image: 1 = Flush hose connector, 2 = Flush hose connector cap, 3 = Garden hose adapter.

3. Connect the garden hose to a water tap, but do not turn the water on yet.

4. Make sure the area around the boat is clear, then start the engine. Immediately after the engine starts, fully turn on the water supply. Confirm that water flows continuously from the jet thrust nozzle and the cooling water pilot outlet.

Notice: Never turn on the water before starting the engine. Water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase and cause severe engine damage. Turn the water on immediately after the engine starts to prevent overheating.

5. Run the engine at a fast idle for 3 to 5 minutes. If the engine stops during flushing, turn off the water supply immediately, then repeat the procedure from step 4.

6. Turn off the water supply. Then drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately moving the remote control lever up to half throttle and back to idle for 10 to 15 seconds.

7. Stop the engine.

Notice: Never keep the water on when the engine is not running. Also, after the water supply has been turned off, do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds to avoid overheating.

8. Remove the garden hose adapter from the flush hose connector.

9. Reinstall the flush hose connector cap and tighten it securely.

If your boat has twin engines, repeat the same flushing procedure for the other engine.,['Manual09_257', 'Manual09_258']

To replace a blown fuse on the boat, please follow the steps below. Before starting, make sure the engines are off and the boat is secure.

WARNING: Do not install a fuse with a higher amperage than specified. Using an incorrectly rated fuse can damage the electrical system and may cause a fire. Always replace a blown fuse with a fuse of the correct amperage.

1. Open the engine hood.

2. Unfasten the band that secures the fuse box area.

<PIC>

1 Band

3. Remove the fuse box from the air filter case by pulling the fuse box toward the bow.

4. Remove the fuse box cover. Push both sides of the fuse box cover inward, then pull the cover toward the bow to take it off.

<PIC>

1 Fuse box cover

5. Identify the fuse that needs to be replaced. The fuse box layout is shown below, including the fuse puller and spare fuses.

<PIC>

1 Electronic throttle valve fuse  
2 Fuel pump fuse  
3 Main relay drive fuse  
4 Main fuse  
5 Fuse puller  
6 Battery fuse  
7 Spare fuse  
8 Unused fuse

6. If you need to replace the accessory fuse or bilge pump fuse, open the battery compartment and remove the fuse holder to access them.

<PIC>

1 Fuse holder  
2 Accessory fuse  
3 Bilge pump fuse

7. Use the fuse puller to remove the blown fuse, then install a spare fuse with the correct amperage.

Fuse amperage ratings:

Electronic throttle valve fuse: 10 A  
Fuel pump fuse: 10 A  
Main relay drive fuse: 10 A  
Main fuse: 20 A  
Battery fuse: 30 A  
Accessory fuse: 20 A  
Bilge pump fuse: 3 A

8. Reinstall the fuse box cover.

9. If the accessory fuse or bilge pump fuse was replaced, reinstall the fuse holder.

10. Reinstall the fuse box onto the air filter case.

11. Fasten the band securely.

12. Close the engine hood.

If the new fuse blows again shortly after replacement, there may be an electrical fault. In that case, have the electrical system inspected by a qualified Boat Dealer before operating the boat again.,['Manual09_281', 'Manual09_282', 'Manual09_283', 'Manual09_284']

To check the engine oil level on your boat, follow the procedure below carefully. The boat has two engines, so check one engine first, then repeat the same process for the other engine.

Important safety notes before you start:

WARNING: Engine oil can be extremely hot immediately after the engines are turned off. Avoid contact with the oil or getting oil on your clothes, as this could cause burns.

NOTICE:
- Do not run the engine with too much or too little oil in the oil tank. Either condition can damage the engine.
- Keep debris and water out of the oil tank filler hole. Contamination in the engine oil can cause serious engine damage.

Tips:
- If checking the oil level on land, the engine must be running while water is being supplied to the cooling water passages.
- If checking the oil level on the water, moor the boat securely so it will not drift.

Steps to check the engine oil level:

1. With the engine stopped, place the boat in a precisely level position on land, or launch the boat.

2. Look in all directions to confirm it is safe, then start the engine.

3. Run the engine at idle speed for at least 6 minutes. If the ambient temperature is 20°C / 68°F or lower, idle the engine for an additional 5 minutes.

4. Stop the engine.

5. Open the engine hood.

6. Locate the oil tank filler cap/dipstick on the engine. Loosen and remove the oil tank filler cap, then wipe the attached dipstick clean.

<PIC>

1 Oil tank filler cap/Dipstick

7. Reinsert the oil tank filler cap by screwing it into the filler hole until it stops. Then remove it again and check the oil level on the dipstick. The oil level should be between the minimum and maximum level marks.

<PIC>

1 Dipstick  
2 Maximum level mark  
3 Minimum level mark

8. If the oil level is significantly above the maximum level mark, have the boat checked by a Boat Dealer. If the oil level is below the minimum level mark, slowly add engine oil.

9. Repeat the wipe, insert, remove, and check process until the oil level is within the proper range.

10. Securely reinstall the oil tank filler cap and turn it until it stops.

11. Repeat the same oil level check procedure for the other engine.

12. Close the engine hood securely.

The correct oil level is between the minimum and maximum marks on the dipstick. Do not operate the boat if the oil level is too low or excessively high.,['Manual09_196', 'Manual09_197']

The coffee machine has an energy saving mode to help reduce power use when it is not being used. By default, it will automatically switch off after 9 minutes of inactivity. To turn the machine back on, press either the Espresso button or the Lungo button.



If you want to switch the machine off before the automatic power-off time, press the Espresso and Lungo buttons at the same time.



You can also change the automatic power-off setting by following these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds.



2. The Espresso button will blink to show the current energy saving setting.



3. Press the Espresso button to select the setting you want:
- Press once to set automatic power off after 9 minutes.
- Press one more time to set automatic power off after 30 minutes.
- Press one more time to deactivate the automatic power-off function.



4. After selecting the desired setting, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds to exit energy saving mode and save the setting.



After this, the machine will use the power-off option you selected.,['Manual07_4', 'Manual07_5', 'Manual07_6', 'Manual07_7', 'Manual07_8', 'Manual07_9']

To change the default energy saving setting, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds to enter the energy saving setting mode.

<PIC>

2. The Espresso button will blink. The blinking indicates the current automatic power-off setting.

<PIC>

3. Press the Espresso button to switch between the available settings:

- Press once to set automatic power-off after 9 minutes.
- Press one more time to set automatic power-off after 30 minutes.
- Press one more time to deactivate automatic power-off.

Each press moves to the next option.

<PIC>

4. Once your preferred setting is selected, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds to save the setting and exit energy saving setting mode.

<PIC>

After this, the machine will use the selected energy saving setting going forward.,['Manual07_6', 'Manual07_7', 'Manual07_8', 'Manual07_9']

To program the water volume of your coffee machine, please follow these steps:

1. Turn the machine on by pressing either the Espresso or Lungo button. Wait until the lights stop blinking and remain steady. Steady lights mean the machine is heated and ready.

<PIC>

2. Fill the water tank with fresh drinking water. Then lift the lever, insert a capsule, and close the lever completely.

<PIC>

3. Place a cup under the coffee outlet. Use a cup large enough for the amount of coffee you want to program.

<PIC>

4. Press and hold the button you want to program: Espresso for a smaller cup or Lungo for a larger cup. Keep holding the button while the coffee is flowing.

<PIC>

5. When the cup has reached your desired volume, release the button.

6. The selected water volume is now saved for that button. The next time you press that same Espresso or Lungo button, the machine will automatically stop at the programmed volume.

Please do not lift the lever while the machine is operating, as hot water or coffee may cause scalding.,['Manual07_24', 'Manual07_25', 'Manual07_26', 'Manual07_27']

To empty the system before a long period of non-use, for frost protection, or before maintenance, please follow the steps below.

Please note: after the emptying process is completed, the machine will be blocked for approximately 10 minutes and cannot be used during this time.

1. Turn the machine off by pressing the Espresso and Lungo buttons at the same time.
<PIC>

2. Remove the water tank, then lift/open the lever fully.
<PIC>

3. Press and hold both the Espresso and Lungo buttons for 3 seconds to start emptying mode.
<PIC>

4. The two button lights will blink alternately, indicating that emptying mode has been activated.
<PIC>

5. Close the lever to begin the emptying process.
<PIC>

6. The machine will switch off automatically once the process is finished.

7. After the machine has switched off, empty and clean the used capsule container and drip tray.

For safety, allow the machine to cool before handling removable parts, and do not unplug or move the machine while the emptying process is running.,['Manual07_28', 'Manual07_29', 'Manual07_30', 'Manual07_31', 'Manual07_32']

To reset your coffee machine to its factory settings, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Lungo button for 5 seconds.

<PIC>

2. The LEDs will blink quickly 3 times. This confirms that the machine has been successfully reset to factory settings.

<PIC>

3. After the reset, the LEDs will continue blinking normally while the machine heats up. Once the lights become steady, the machine is ready to use again.

<PIC>

After the reset, the default factory settings will be restored as follows:

- Espresso cup volume: 40 ml / 1.35 oz
- Lungo cup volume: 110 ml / 3.7 oz
- Automatic power-off mode: 9 minutes,['Manual07_33', 'Manual07_34', 'Manual07_35']

To help your coffee maker stay in good condition and keep the coffee quality consistent, please clean it after use with the steps below:

1. After brewing, remove your cup first. Then lift and close the lever once to eject the used capsule into the used capsule container.

<PIC>

2. Empty the used capsule container and drip tray. These parts can be removed separately, which makes it easier to rinse away coffee residue and water collected after brewing. Dry the parts before placing them back into the machine.

<PIC>

3. Before wiping or cleaning the machine body, unplug the machine and let it cool down. For safety, never immerse the appliance or any part of it in water, and do not put the machine or its removable parts in a dishwasher.

4. Use only a soft damp cloth and, if needed, a mild cleaning agent for the exterior surfaces. Do not use strong cleaning agents, solvent cleaners, sharp objects, hard brushes, or abrasive pads, as these may damage the finish or internal parts.

5. Clean the coffee outlet regularly with a soft damp cloth. This helps prevent coffee residue buildup around the outlet and supports smooth coffee flow over time.

<PIC>

Following these simple cleaning steps after use can help reduce residue buildup, protect the machine, and support long-lasting performance.,['Manual07_23', 'Manual07_48', 'Manual07_47']

The swim platform itself is the stern platform area and does not open as a whole. The part that can be opened is the rear platform hatch, which gives access to the wet storage compartment under the swim platform.

<PIC>

To open the wet storage compartment under the swim platform:

1. Locate the rear platform hatch on the swim platform area.
2. Pull the lock handle up.
3. Turn the lock handle clockwise.
4. Lift/open the rear platform hatch.

<PIC>

After the hatch is opened, you can access the wet storage compartment underneath.

<PIC>

To close it, lower the rear platform hatch, turn the lock handle counterclockwise until the hatch is securely closed, and then push the lock handle down.

Important safety note: Stay away from the swim platform area while the engine is running. Exhaust gases from underneath the platform contain carbon monoxide, which is colorless and odorless and can cause serious injury or death if inhaled. Also, the engines will not start if the rear platform hatch is not securely closed.,['Manual09_168', 'Manual09_143', 'Manual09_144']

The livewell is located on the starboard side of the boat at the stern. You can open it from the stern-side livewell lid, then use the livewell switch at the helm switch panel to fill it with water.

To open the livewell:
1. Locate the livewell lid at the starboard stern area.
2. Pull the latch to release the lid.
3. Lift the livewell lid open.

<PIC>

In the image above: 1 is the livewell lid, and 2 is the latch.

After opening the lid, you will see the livewell compartment inside.

<PIC>

To add water to the livewell:
1. Go to the switch panel and push the livewell switch to turn on the livewell pump. This will supply water to the livewell.
2. Watch the water level in the livewell. When there is enough water, push the livewell switch again to turn the pump off.
3. If you want to keep the water aerated and circulating, use the aerator switch. The aerator can help maintain water movement for live bait or fish.

<PIC>

In the switch panel image: 1 is the aerator switch, and 2 is the livewell switch.

Tip: If you need light inside the livewell, push the courtesy light switch. This turns on the livewell light.,['Manual09_153', 'Manual09_154', 'Manual09_155']

The maintenance setting screen on the boat controller shows the engine operating time since the last maintenance was completed. It displays separate hour counters for the port engine and the starboard engine, so you can quickly check how long each engine has been running since the previous maintenance reset.

<PIC>

After maintenance has been performed, you can reset the maintenance hour count from this screen. This reset should be done only after the required maintenance work has actually been completed.

To reset the operating hours:

1. Tap the “Reset” button at the bottom of the maintenance setting screen.

<PIC>

2. A confirmation message will appear. Tap “YES” to confirm and reset the number of operating hours.

If you do not want to reset the hours, tap “NO” to return to the maintenance setting screen without making any change.

<PIC>,['Manual09_83', 'Manual09_84', 'Manual09_85']

At the steering position, the factory reset screen appears on the multi-function display. It shows the message “Reset all parameters to factory setting?” in the center of the screen.

Below the message are two touch buttons: “Reset” on the left, used to start restoring the settings to the factory defaults, and “Back” on the right, used to return without proceeding.

<PIC>,['Manual09_87']

The trip screen is a page on the multi-function display that shows trip-related operating information, such as engine operating hours, fuel consumption, trip distance, fuel used, speed data, and water temperature.

<PIC>

The main parts of the trip screen are:
1. Menu: Displays the trip information items.
2. Scrollbar: Used to move through the available menu items.
3. “Reset” button: Used to reset resettable trip data.

The menu shows 4 items at one time. Use the scrollbar on the right side of the menu to view additional items.

<PIC>

Items that may appear on the trip screen include water temperature, average fuel consumption, trip distance, fuel used, fuel flow, highest speed, average speed, and engine hours.

Please note: water temperature, fuel flow, and engine hours cannot be reset.

To reset a single resettable item, touch and hold that item for several seconds. To reset all resettable display items at once, tap the “Reset” button.,['Manual09_69', 'Manual09_70']

When you are ready to get underway and the area around the boat is clear, move both remote control levers forward together from the neutral position. This shifts the boat into forward. As you continue moving the levers farther forward, the engines accelerate and produce more jet thrust, so the boat moves forward faster.

<PIC>

At first, moving the levers forward passes through the shift range. Once the levers are moved farther into the throttle range, the engines increase speed and forward thrust increases. For normal operation, move both levers together and adjust them so both engines run at the same engine speed.

When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates lift fully up and uncover the jet thrust nozzles. This directs all jet thrust to the rear, which pushes the boat forward.

<PIC>

Before shifting or applying throttle, always confirm there are no swimmers, boats, docks, or other obstacles near the boat. Also remember that this jet boat needs throttle for steering response, so operate smoothly and keep scanning the water ahead and around you.,['Manual09_46', 'Manual09_50']

You can check the steering system in two stages: first before you launch the boat, and then by confirming proper steering response while operating at a safe speed in open water.

Before driving, perform the steering check while the boat is still on land. Make sure the steering wheel feels secure and is not loose. There should be no free play when you pull/push it in and out, and no excessive looseness when you rotate it. Turn the steering wheel fully to the right and fully to the left, and confirm that it moves smoothly through the entire range without binding, catching, or restriction.

<PIC>

Next, have one person turn the steering wheel while another person observes the stern area. Confirm that both jet thrust nozzles move in the same direction as the steering input: when the wheel is turned right, the nozzles should point to starboard/right; when the wheel is turned left, the nozzles should point to port/left. There should be no looseness or delay between steering wheel movement and nozzle movement. Also check that the articulating keel moves together with the jet thrust nozzles.

<PIC>

When you are driving, remember that this boat steers by jet thrust. Steering response depends on both the steering wheel position and the amount of throttle. More throttle gives more thrust and a sharper turn; less throttle gives less thrust and a wider, more gradual turn. If you pull the remote control levers back to idle or neutral while moving faster than trolling speed, steering ability will quickly decrease. The boat may stop responding to steering wheel input until you apply throttle again or slow to trolling speed.

<PIC>

Important: do not pull the remote control levers back to idle when trying to steer away from an object. You need throttle to steer. If the steering wheel feels loose, the nozzles or articulating keel do not move correctly, or steering does not respond normally, do not operate the boat until the issue has been corrected by a qualified boat dealer.,['Manual09_206', 'Manual09_207', 'Manual09_241']

At least one fire extinguisher should be carried on board, and it must be full and ready for use before operating the boat. Carrying two fire extinguishers is recommended for better coverage.

For the required onboard extinguisher, mount it inside the lockable storage compartment. For this location, a chemical-type fire extinguisher with a capacity of 2 lb or more is recommended. Make sure it is secured in its mounting position so it does not move around while the boat is underway.

<PIC>

If you carry a second fire extinguisher, mount it in the battery compartment. This compartment has a location label outside it to help identify where the extinguisher is stored.

<PIC>

Because this second extinguisher is located near the engine compartment, it should be a clean-agent type designed to displace oxygen using an inert gas, such as CO2 or FE-36(TM). A capacity of 5 lb or more is recommended for this location.

<PIC>

As a quick check before each outing, confirm that the extinguisher is present, full, properly secured, and easy to access. Also verify that the extinguisher type and condition meet applicable boating requirements for your area.,['Manual09_211', 'Manual09_212', 'Manual09_213']

To care for the boat’s throttle cable, apply water-resistant grease to the throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS. This helps the moving parts slide and rotate smoothly and reduces stiffness, wear, and corrosion, especially after exposure to moisture or saltwater.

Before doing this maintenance, make sure the engines are turned off and the boat is secure. Locate the APS pulley wheel area in the engine compartment, then apply a light, even coating of water-resistant grease directly to the exposed throttle-cable inner wire where the arrow indicates in the image below.

<PIC>

After greasing, move the remote control levers gently to confirm the throttle movement feels smooth and returns properly. If the throttle cable feels sticky, does not move freely, or does not return normally after lubrication, have it inspected by a qualified boat technician or Boat Dealer before operating the boat.,['Manual09_263']

When you will not be using the camera for a while, remove the battery pack from the camera and attach the protective cover to the battery. This helps protect the battery contacts and prevents short-circuiting during storage.

<PIC>

Before using the camera again, recharge the battery fully. Use only compatible battery packs such as BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512, and recharge in an environment between 0°C and 40°C / 32°F and 104°F.

To recharge the battery, follow the steps for your charger model:

For CG-580:
Flip out the charger prongs in the direction shown, attach the battery to the charger, and plug the charger directly into a power outlet. Charging starts automatically, and the charge lamp will blink while charging.

<PIC>

For CB-5L:
Attach the battery to the charger, connect the power cord to the charger, then plug the other end into a power outlet. Recharging starts automatically, and the red lamp will blink.

<PIC>

When the red lamp stays lit, the battery has reached about 90% or higher. For a full charge, continue charging for about one more hour after the lamp stays on. A fully exhausted battery typically takes about 100 minutes for BP-511A/BP-514, or about 90 minutes for BP-511/BP-512, before this final top-up period.

After charging is complete, remove the battery from the charger and unplug the charger or power cord from the power outlet. If the battery operating time becomes much shorter even after a full recharge, the battery may have reached the end of its service life and should be replaced.,['Manual10_10', 'Manual10_12', 'Manual10_13']

To install or replace the camera battery, first make sure the camera power switch is set to <OFF>. Then use a fully charged compatible battery pack, such as BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512.

1. Open the battery compartment cover.
Slide the battery compartment lever in the direction shown, then open the cover fully.
<PIC>

2. Insert the battery.
Hold the battery with the electrical contacts facing downward, then push it into the battery compartment until it locks securely in place. Please do not force the battery in the wrong direction or install it with reversed polarity.
<PIC>

3. Close the cover.
Press the battery compartment cover down until it snaps shut. The camera may not operate if the cover is not closed tightly.
<PIC>

After the battery is installed, you can turn the power switch to <ON> to use the camera. If the camera still does not power on, please check that the battery is fully charged, installed correctly, and that the battery compartment cover is completely closed.,['Manual10_16', 'Camera_09', 'Camera_10']

You can power the camera from a household power socket by using the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2. This kit lets you operate the camera without relying on the battery level.

Before connecting, make sure the camera power switch is set to OFF, and use only the specified AC adapter kit.

1. Connect the DC Coupler to the AC adapter.

Insert the DC Coupler plug firmly into the socket on the AC adapter, as shown below.

<PIC>

2. Connect the power cord.

Connect the power cord to the AC adapter first, then insert the plug fully into the household power outlet. When you are finished using the camera, unplug the power cord from the outlet by holding the plug, not the cord.

<PIC>

3. Route the cord through the groove.

Carefully place the cord into the designated groove. Make sure it sits properly in the groove and is not pinched, bent sharply, or damaged.

<PIC>

4. Insert the DC Coupler into the camera.

Open the battery compartment cover, then open the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler into the battery compartment until it locks into place. Route the cord through the notch, then close the battery compartment cover securely.

<PIC>

Important notes:

- Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera power switch is ON.
- Do not handle the power plug with wet hands.
- Keep the cord away from heat sources and avoid scratching, cutting, twisting, or excessively bending it.
- When the camera is not in use, disconnect the power plug from the household outlet.,['Camera_14', 'Camera_15', 'Manual10_19', 'Manual10_20']

To mount the lens before taking photos, follow these steps carefully:

1. Remove the caps.

Take off the rear lens cap from the lens and the body cap from the camera body. Turn each cap in the direction shown by the arrows, then remove it. Try to keep the camera mount facing slightly downward while the caps are off to help reduce dust entering the camera.

<PIC>

2. Attach the lens to the camera.

Find the mount index mark on the lens and match it with the corresponding mark on the camera body:

- For an EF-S lens, align the lens with the white EF-S lens mount index.
- For an EF lens or other non-EF-S lens, align the lens with the red EF lens index mark.

Once the marks are aligned, insert the lens into the mount and turn it in the direction shown by the arrow until it clicks into place. The click means the lens is securely locked on the camera.

<PIC>

3. Set the lens focus mode and remove the front cap.

On the lens, set the focus mode switch to <AF> if you want to use autofocus. If the switch is set to <MF>, autofocus will not operate. After setting the focus mode, squeeze the front lens cap as shown and pull it off.

<PIC>

After these steps, the lens is mounted and the camera is ready for photography.,['Manual10_21', 'Manual10_22', 'Manual10_23']

To install the CF card before taking photos, please follow these steps:

1. Open the CF card slot cover.
Slide the cover in the direction shown by the arrow, then swing it open.

<PIC>

2. Insert the CF card correctly.
Use a compatible CF card. Hold the card with the label side facing toward you, then insert the end with the small holes into the camera slot. Push it in gently and straight until it is fully seated. When the card is inserted properly, the CF card eject button will pop out.

Important: Do not force the card in the wrong direction, as this may damage the card or the camera.

<PIC>

3. Close the cover.
Swing the cover closed, then slide it in the direction shown by the arrow until it snaps securely into place. The camera may not operate properly if the CF card slot cover is not fully closed.

<PIC>

4. Confirm the card is ready.
After the cover is closed, check the camera’s LCD panel. The number of shots remaining should be displayed, which indicates that the camera has recognized the CF card and is ready for photography.

<PIC>,['Manual10_25', 'Manual10_26', 'Manual10_27', 'Manual10_28']

The available information does not provide a procedure for removing the camera shutter button itself. Since the shutter button is an integrated camera control, removing it may damage the camera body or affect shooting operation, so we do not recommend attempting to take it off yourself. If the shutter button is stuck, loose, or not responding, it is best to have the camera checked by an authorized repair service.

For normal use, the shutter button works in two stages:

1. Press the shutter button halfway to activate autofocus and automatic exposure. The camera sets the shutter speed and aperture, and the exposure information appears on the top LCD panel and in the viewfinder.

<PIC>

2. Press the shutter button completely to release the shutter and take the picture.

<PIC>

For smoother operation and to reduce blur, place your fingertip gently on the shutter button, hold the camera steady with your right hand, and press the button smoothly instead of pushing it abruptly. If you press it fully without first pressing halfway, the camera may take a brief moment before capturing the image.,['Manual10_31', 'Manual10_32']

The camera’s date/time back-up battery keeps the date and time settings while the main battery is removed. Its service life is about 5 years. If the date/time resets after replacing or removing the main battery, replace the back-up battery with a new CR2016 lithium battery. After replacement, the date/time setting will be reset, so please set the correct date and time again.

1. Turn the camera’s power switch to <OFF>. Then open the battery compartment cover and remove the main battery.

<PIC>

2. Pull out the date/time battery holder from inside the battery compartment.

<PIC>

3. Remove the old CR2016 battery from the holder and install a new CR2016 lithium battery. Make sure the + and - sides are facing the correct direction before inserting it back.

<PIC>

4. Close the cover securely. Once the battery has been replaced, turn the camera back on and set the correct date and time.

<PIC>,['Manual10_47', 'Manual10_48', 'Manual10_49', 'Manual10_50']

To focus on a subject that is not in the center of the frame, use focus lock. This lets you focus first, keep that focus distance locked, and then recompose the shot before taking the picture.

1. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP.

2. Select the AF point you want to use.

3. Place the selected AF point over the subject, then press the shutter button halfway to focus.

<PIC>

4. Keep the shutter button pressed halfway. While holding it, recompose the picture so the subject is positioned where you want in the frame.

5. Press the shutter button fully to take the picture.

Please note: Focus lock works only in One-Shot AF mode. If the camera is set to AI Servo AF, or if AI Focus AF has switched to Servo operation, focus lock will not work because the camera continues adjusting focus while the shutter button is held halfway.,['Manual10_107']

Use the eyepiece cover when your eye is not against the viewfinder during shooting, such as when using the self-timer or a remote switch. If the viewfinder is left uncovered, stray light can enter through the eyepiece and may affect the exposure of the photo.

To attach the eyepiece cover, follow these steps:

1. Remove the eyecup.

Hold the eyecup from the bottom and push it upward to slide it off the viewfinder.

<PIC>

2. Attach the eyepiece cover.

Take the eyepiece cover from the camera strap, align it with the eyepiece groove, and slide it downward until it is securely in place. This blocks light from entering the viewfinder while the picture is being taken.

<PIC>

After shooting, you can remove the eyepiece cover and reattach the eyecup if you want to use the viewfinder normally again.,['Manual10_154', 'Manual10_155']

Your question likely means: “How do I select the AF Mode before taking a picture?”

You can select the AF mode before shooting by following these steps:

1. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode.

AF mode selection is available in Creative Zone modes, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP. In Basic Zone modes, the camera automatically chooses the AF mode for you.

2. Set the lens focus mode switch to <AF>.

Make sure the switch on the lens is set to AF, not MF. If it is set to MF, autofocus mode selection will not apply.

<PIC>

3. Press the <AF-WB> button.

This opens the AF mode selection on the camera’s LCD panel.

<PIC>

4. Select the AF mode.

While looking at the LCD panel, turn the Main Dial until the AF mode you want appears.

You can choose from:

ONESHOT: One-Shot AF, best for still subjects. Focus locks when you press the shutter button halfway.

AIFOCUS: AI Focus AF, useful when the subject may start moving. The camera can automatically switch from One-Shot AF to AI Servo AF if movement is detected.

AISERVO: AI Servo AF, best for moving subjects. The camera continues to adjust focus while you keep the shutter button pressed halfway.

After selecting the AF mode, press the shutter button halfway to focus, then fully press it to take the picture.,['Manual10_99', 'Camera_30']

In this mode, the camera automatically sets the shutter speed and aperture according to the subject's brightness, making it useful when you want automatic exposure while still being able to adjust more shooting settings than in Full Auto mode. To set it: 1. Turn the camera's Mode Dial. 2. Align the dial with

Yes. You can view captured images on a TV by connecting the camera to the TV with the provided video cable. This is useful when you want to check the image on a larger screen for better visibility.

Before connecting, please turn off both the camera and the TV.

1. Connect the camera to the TV
- Open the camera's terminal cover.
- Connect one end of the provided video cable to the camera's video terminal.
- Connect the other end to the TV monitor's VIDEO IN terminal.
- Make sure the cable plugs are inserted all the way in.
- Turn on the TV, then switch the TV input/source to Video IN.

<PIC>

2. Display the image on the TV
- Turn on the camera.
- Press the playback button on the camera.
- The image will appear on the TV screen.
- At this time, nothing will be displayed on the camera's LCD monitor.

<PIC>

3. After viewing
When you are finished, turn off the camera and the TV first, then disconnect the video cable.

If the image does not display correctly, please check that the camera's [Video system] setting matches your TV's video format. Also, depending on the TV model, the edges of the image may be slightly cut off on the screen.,['Manual10_182', 'Manual10_183']

Yes. You can delete just one photo directly from the camera during image playback. Please follow these steps:

1. Press the playback button to display the saved images on the LCD monitor.

<PIC>

2. Turn the dial to find the photo you want to delete.

3. Press the erase button. The erase menu will appear at the bottom of the screen.

<PIC>

4. Turn the dial to select [Erase], then press SET to confirm. The access lamp will blink while the camera deletes the selected image.

<PIC>

If you want to delete more photos one by one, repeat steps 2 to 4 for each image.

Please note: once an image is erased, it cannot be recovered. Make sure you no longer need the photo before deleting it. If the image is protected, it cannot be erased until you cancel its protection first.,['Manual10_184', 'Manual10_185', 'Manual10_186']

Yes. You can erase all images on the CF card at one time. Please note that protected images will not be erased by this operation.

1. Enter playback mode.
Press the playback button to display an image on the camera screen.

2. Open the erase menu.
Press the erase button with the trash can icon. The Erase menu will appear at the bottom of the screen.

<PIC>

3. Select [All].
Turn the dial to highlight [All], then press SET. A confirmation screen will appear.

<PIC>

4. Confirm the deletion.
Turn the dial to select [OK], then press SET. The camera will erase all unprotected images on the CF card.

<PIC>

Important: Once images are erased, they cannot be recovered. Please make sure you no longer need them before confirming. If some images remain after using [All], they are likely protected. To delete protected images as well, you will need to cancel their protection first, or format the CF card if you want to completely clear everything on the card. Formatting will erase all data, including protected images, so transfer any important files to a computer before doing that.,['Camera_58', 'Camera_59', 'Manual10_187']

To print photos from the camera using the CP Direct method, please use a Canon printer that supports CP Direct, then operate the printing process from the camera’s LCD monitor.

1. Connect the camera and printer

First, turn off both the camera and the printer. Use the suitable direct-printing cable for your printer type. For a printer that supports CP Direct, use the cable specified for that printer. Connect the cable to the camera’s DIGITAL terminal, making sure the plug is inserted in the correct direction, then connect the other end to the printer.

<PIC>

When connecting the cable to the camera, the cable plug’s icon should face the front side of the camera.

<PIC>

2. Turn on the printer and camera, then display the photo

After connecting the cable, turn on the printer, then turn on the camera. Press the playback button to display the photos stored on the CF card. When the camera recognizes the printer, a printer connection icon will appear in the upper-left area of the LCD monitor.

For CP Direct printing, confirm that the CP Direct icon is shown. The camera may display different icons depending on the printer connection type, so checking the icon helps confirm that you are using the correct direct printing method.

<PIC>

<PIC>

3. Select the photo you want to print

Turn the main dial to browse through the images on the CF card. When the photo you want to print appears on the LCD monitor, press the SET button to open the print setting screen.

4. Adjust the print settings as needed

On the print setting screen, set the available options such as the number of copies, print style, date imprinting, or trimming if you want to change the printed area. The exact options shown may vary depending on the connected CP Direct printer and paper cassette.

If you are printing index images with CP Direct, the number of images printed on one index sheet depends on the paper size:

Credit card size: 20 images
L size: 42 images
Postcard size: 63 images

5. Start printing

After confirming the settings, select [Print], then press SET. Printing will start. When printing is complete, the camera returns to the previous print screen.

To stop printing while it is in progress, press SET while [Stop] is displayed, select [OK], then press SET again.

Additional notes:

Make sure the camera battery is fully charged before printing, or use a stable power source if available.
Do not disconnect the cable while printing is in progress.
Before disconnecting the cable, turn off both the camera and the printer, then pull the cable out by holding the plug, not the cord.
If printing is stopped and you want to continue later, use [Resume] when available. However, printing may not resume if the print order was changed, an image scheduled for printing was erased, the paper cassette was changed during CP Direct index printing, or the CF card has very little remaining capacity.,['Manual10_191', 'Manual10_192', 'Manual10_193', 'Manual10_195']

To fine-tune the camera viewfinder for your eyesight, adjust the dioptric adjustment knob. This helps make the AF points inside the viewfinder appear sharp, so you can see the focus area clearly while shooting.

Steps:
1. Look through the viewfinder.
2. Turn the dioptric adjustment knob left or right.
3. Stop when the AF points in the viewfinder look clear and sharp to your eyes.

<PIC>

The position shown in the picture is the standard setting, which is approximately -1 dpt.

If you turn the knob through its adjustment range and the viewfinder still does not look sharp, you can use an optional Dioptric Adjustment Lens E. There are 10 types available, so you can choose one that better matches your eyesight.,['Manual10_54']

Once you receive the Active Noise Canceling Truly Wireless Earphones, you should have the main earphones set and charging case as shown below:

<PIC>

The earphones include the following parts:

1. Left and right earbuds
Each earbud is marked with “L” or “R” so you can easily identify which side to wear.

<PIC>

2. Multifunction buttons
Both earbuds have a multifunction button on the outer surface. These buttons are used for common controls such as music playback, calls, pairing, voice assistant, and other earbud functions.

<PIC>

3. Status LED indicators
Each earbud has a status LED indicator. These lights help show charging, pairing, and connection status.

4. Charging case with battery level LED indicators
The charging case stores and charges the earbuds. The front of the case has battery level LED indicators, which show the case’s remaining power.

<PIC>

5. Battery level display button
There is also a battery level display button on the charging case. Pressing it lets you check the case battery level.

<PIC>

So, in summary, you should have: the left earbud, the right earbud, and the charging case. The earbuds include multifunction buttons and status LED indicators, while the case includes battery level LED indicators and a battery level display button.,['Manual12_0', 'Manual12_2', 'Manual12_1', 'Manual12_3', 'Manual12_4']

To charge the earphones charging case when its battery is low, please follow these steps:

1. Connect the USB-C end of the charging cable to the charging port on the back of the charging case.
<PIC>

2. Connect the standard USB end of the cable to a suitable USB power source, such as a USB wall adapter, computer USB port, or power bank.
<PIC>

3. While the case is charging, the Battery Level LED Indicators on the front of the case will show the current charge level:
- 0-24%: one blue LED flashes
- 25-49%: one solid blue LED and one flashing blue LED
- 50-74%: two solid blue LEDs and one flashing blue LED
- 75-99%: three solid blue LEDs and one flashing blue LED
- 100%: four solid blue LEDs stay on
<PIC>

4. You can also check the charging case battery level at any time by pressing the Battery Level Display Button on the right side of the case.
<PIC>

For best results, keep the charging cable and case well ventilated while charging, and make sure the case and earphones are dry before connecting them to power.,['earphones_04', 'earphones_05', 'earphones_06', 'earphones_07']

When Bluetooth pairing and connecting, you may encounter the following scenarios:

1. Earbuds power on and enter pairing mode

When you open the charging case, the earbuds will first connect to each other. After the left and right earbuds are linked, they will automatically enter Bluetooth pairing mode and become available for connection.

2. Earbuds are still in the charging case

When the earbuds are inside the case, their LED indicators show the charging or battery status instead of the Bluetooth connection status:

<PIC>

- Solid red light: low battery
- Flashing light blue light: charging
- Solid blue light: fully charged

3. Earbuds are removed from the charging case

Once the earbuds are taken out of the case, the LED indicators show the Bluetooth pairing or connection status:

<PIC>

- Flashing light blue light: the earbuds are in pairing mode and ready to connect
- Solid light blue light: the earbuds are connected successfully

4. Connecting from your phone or device

On your phone, tablet, or computer, go to the Bluetooth settings page and make sure Bluetooth is turned ON. Then select the earbuds from the available device list to connect. The device name may appear as “earphones” or “AL Hydrabuds ANC,” depending on the device display.

<PIC>

Please note: If the earbuds were previously paired with a nearby device, they may automatically reconnect to that device when powered on. If you want to pair them with a different device, turn Bluetooth off on the previously connected device first, or disconnect the earbuds from that device so they can enter pairing mode again.

5. Multipoint Bluetooth connection

You may also encounter multipoint connection. This feature allows the earbuds to connect with up to two devices, such as a smartphone and a computer.

To use multipoint connection:

- First connect the earbuds to your first device.
- Turn Bluetooth OFF on the first device so the earbuds disconnect and become available for pairing.
- Pair the earbuds with your second device.
- Turn Bluetooth back ON on the first device. The earbuds should reconnect automatically.

After this, both devices can be paired, and you can use either one for music or calls.,['Manual12_5', 'Manual12_6', 'Manual12_7']

The two main controlling functions of the earphones are controlling music and controlling calls.

1. Controlling Music
You can use the multifunction buttons on the earbuds to manage music playback, volume, and track selection:
• Play/Pause: Press either the left or right earbud once.
• Volume Up: Press the left earbud twice.
• Volume Down: Press the right earbud twice.
• Previous Track: Press the left earbud three times.
• Next Track: Press the right earbud three times.

2. Controlling Calls
You can also handle phone calls directly from the earbuds:
• Answer a call: Press either the left or right earbud.
• End a call: Press and hold either the left or right earbud.
• Reject a call: Press and hold either the left or right earbud when a call is coming in.
,['Manual12_8', 'Manual12_9']

Besides controlling music playback and calls, you can also use the earphones to operate several additional functions through the multifunction buttons:

1. Voice Assistant
To activate your phone’s voice assistant, press and hold the earbud button until you hear the first beep, then release it.
<PIC>

2. Music App

If your earphones are not working properly, you can reset them using the steps below. Before resetting, make sure both earbuds are removed from the charging case and disconnected from Bluetooth on your phone or other connected device.

For a factory reset, press and hold the multifunction buttons on both earbuds at the same time for 6 seconds. This will erase the earphones' saved settings and Bluetooth pairing information, so you may need to pair them with your device again afterward.

<PIC>

If the earbuds are frozen, locked up, or completely unresponsive, you can perform a hardware reset instead. Press and hold the multifunction buttons on both earbuds at the same time for 10 seconds. After the reset, press the buttons again to power the earbuds back on.

A hardware reset is only needed in rare cases when the earbuds do not respond normally. For most connection or pairing issues, try the factory reset first.,['Manual12_11', 'Manual12_12']

Maintenance and care policies for the earphones:

<PIC>

To keep your Active Noise Canceling Truly Wireless Earphones and charging case in good condition, please follow these care guidelines:

1. Clean gently and keep dry
Use a soft, clean cloth or paper towel to wipe the earphones and charging case. Do not use harsh chemicals, detergents, sprays, or abrasive cleaners, as they may damage the surface, speaker mesh, charging contacts, or internal components. Always make sure the earphones and charging case are completely dry before charging.

2. Store in a cool, dry place
When the earphones are not in use, place them back in the charging case and store them in a cool, dry environment. Avoid leaving them in damp areas, bathrooms, direct sunlight, or places with heavy dust.

3. Handle charging cables carefully
When connecting or disconnecting the charging cable from the charging case, hold the plug firmly and insert or remove it gently. Do not tug, yank, twist, or pull on the cable while it is connected, as this may damage the cable or the charging port.

4. Avoid extreme temperatures
Do not expose the earphones or charging case to high heat, fire, direct sunlight for long periods, or extreme cold. Excessive temperature exposure may affect battery performance, charging safety, and product lifespan.

5. Avoid impact and excessive force
Do not drop, crush, or apply strong pressure to the earphones or charging case. Physical damage may affect sound quality, charging, pairing, or button operation.

6. Dispose of or recycle properly
When the earphones are no longer usable, please recycle or dispose of them according to your local laws and regulations. Because the product contains a lithium polymer battery, contact your local recycling facility or the product manufacturer if you need guidance on proper disposal.,['Manual12_13']

When using this eReader, you may occasionally run into power, file transfer, audio playback, Micro SD card, or general operating issues. You can use the checks below to identify the problem and try the appropriate solution.

<PIC>

1. The eReader cannot power on

- First, charge the device with the USB cable until the battery indicator is full. If it is the first time you are using the reader, it may need a full charge before it can start normally.
- Press and hold the Power button for about 3-5 seconds to turn it on.
- If the device still does not power on after being fully charged, the unit may be defective. In that case, please contact the manufacturer or seller for further assistance.

2. Files cannot be downloaded or transferred to the reader

- Check whether the USB cable is working properly. A damaged or charge-only USB cable may prevent file transfer.
- After connecting the eReader to your computer, the screen should show “USB Connection,” and the computer should detect it as a removable disk.
- If a Micro SD card is inserted, the computer may show two removable disks: one for the built-in memory and one for the Micro SD card.
- Make sure the eReader’s internal memory or Micro SD card is not full. If storage is full, delete unused files or move files to another storage device.
- If you are using a very old Windows system such as Windows 98, a USB 2.0 driver may be required for the computer to recognize the eReader as a removable disk.

3. Music files cannot be played

- The eReader can play most common audio formats, but some newer music formats, special encodings, or high-resolution files may not be supported.
- Try using another music file in a common format to confirm whether the issue is with a specific file.
- If only certain files fail to play, convert them to a more common compatible audio format and try again.
- If no music files can be played, please contact technical support for further help.

4. The device cannot recognize the inserted Micro SD card

- Turn off the eReader first.
- Remove the Micro SD card, then insert it again securely.
- Turn the device back on and check whether the card is detected.
- Always power off the device before inserting or removing the Micro SD card.
- Do not reset the device while the Micro SD card is inserted, as this may cause recognition or data issues.
- The device supports Micro SD/SDHC cards up to 16GB, so please also confirm that the card capacity is within the supported range.

5. The device still does not work properly after normal operation

- If the issue is minor, you may try using the reset switch on the back of the device near the card reader to restore normal operation. Remove the Micro SD card before resetting.
- Do not attempt to open, repair, or disassemble the reader yourself.
- Please contact the manufacturer or seller if the device has been exposed to liquid, got wet, was damaged by dropping, shows obvious overheating, or still cannot work properly after you have followed the correct operating steps.

To help prevent problems, keep the eReader dry, avoid extreme temperatures, do not drop or bend it, and clean it only with a soft, dry cloth.,['Manual13_0']

To properly complete the fax connection procedure, please follow these safety and connection steps:

1. Place the fax machine in a safe location
Make sure the product is on a flat, stable surface near both a telephone wall jack and an easily accessible AC power outlet. Keep cables arranged so they do not create a tripping hazard, and do not block access to the power outlet.

2. Use the correct telephone connection
Connect the fax machine to the telephone line using a standard modular telephone jack, USOC RJ11C. Use the compliant telephone cord and modular plug provided with the product, and connect it only to a compatible modular jack that also meets the applicable requirements.

3. Connect power safely
The product should be connected only to an AC power source that matches the rating label on the machine. Do not connect it to a DC power source or inverter. If you are unsure about the power source, please contact a qualified electrician.

4. Keep emergency access available
The product must be installed near an AC power outlet that is easy to reach. In an emergency, unplug the power cord from the AC outlet to shut off the power completely.

5. Disconnect cables before installation, service, or changes
To reduce the risk of electrical shock, always disconnect all cables from the wall outlet before installing, servicing, or modifying the equipment. For fax models, unplug the telephone line cord first, then unplug the power cord from the AC outlet, and disconnect any other connected cables if applicable.

<PIC>

Important safety reminders:
- Do not install or modify telephone wiring during a thunderstorm.
- Do not install a telephone wall jack in a wet or damp location.
- Never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line has been unplugged from the wall jack.
- Use only a No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord.
- If liquid, metal objects, smoke, unusual heat, or strong odors are noticed, unplug the product immediately and contact authorized service personnel.,['Manual15_2']

To help ensure your safety when using this fax, please pay attention to the following precautions:

1. General use and placement
- Read and follow all warnings and instructions marked on the product, and keep the safety information for future reference.
- Place the fax on a flat, level, stable surface that is free from vibration and shocks.
- Keep it near an easily accessible AC power outlet so you can unplug it quickly in an emergency.
- Make sure power cords, telephone cords, and other cables are secured so they do not create a tripping hazard.
- Do not place heavy objects on the product, and do not place objects around it that could block access to the power outlet.
- Do not place the product on an unstable cart, stand, or table, especially where children may be nearby.

2. Electrical and fire safety
Failure to follow electrical safety precautions may create a risk of electric shock or fire. Please note the following:
- Do not use the product during a thunderstorm.
- If the product has been dropped, the casing is damaged, or liquid or metal objects get inside the product, unplug it from the AC power outlet immediately and contact authorized service personnel.
- If the product becomes unusually hot, releases smoke, gives off a strong smell, or has liquid spilled on it, unplug it immediately and arrange service.
- Connect the product only to the type of AC power source indicated on the product rating label. Do not connect it to a DC power source or inverter.

<PIC>

3. Cleaning precautions
To reduce the risk of fire, do not use flammable substances, sprays, or organic solvents/liquids containing alcohol or ammonia to clean the inside or outside of the product. Use only a dry, lint-free cloth for cleaning.

<PIC>

4. Power cord safety
- Use only the power cord provided with the product, if one was included.
- Do not allow anything to rest on the power cord.
- Do not place the product where people may step on the cord.
- Do not position the product so the cord is stretched, pinched, or strained.
- Do not use the product if the power cord is frayed or damaged.
- Do not pull on the middle of the AC power cord; unplug it by holding the plug.
- Do not use extension cords.
- Do not use cables that do not meet the required specifications.

5. Fax and telephone line safety
For models with fax/telephone functions:
- Do not use the product near water or in places that may become wet, such as near sinks, washing machines, wet basements, or swimming pools.
- Avoid using the fax/telephone function during a thunderstorm.
- Do not use this product to report a gas leak while you are near the leak.
- Use only a No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord.
- When installing or changing telephone lines, do not touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line has been disconnected from the wall jack.
- Do not install telephone wiring during a thunderstorm or install a telephone wall jack in a wet location.

6. Paper tray and moving-part safety
- Wait until printed pages or received faxes have fully exited the product before picking them up, otherwise your fingers may be caught inside the machine.
- Do not put your hand or any foreign object into the ink insertion slot.
- If the paper tray is extended to hold large paper, position the product so the tray does not protrude beyond the edge of the desk or table. If someone bumps the tray, the product could fall and cause injury.

<PIC>

7. Avoid finger injuries
Be careful not to place your fingers in the areas shown below, especially near covers, hinges, trays, and moving parts. These areas may pinch your fingers or cause injury during opening, closing, or operation.

<PIC>

When changing the tray size from a large paper size back to the original size, keep your fingers away from the gaps or slots at the bottom of the tray to avoid pinching.

<PIC>

8. Service and repair
Do not attempt to service or repair this product yourself. Opening or removing covers may expose you to dangerous voltage points and other risks, and may affect warranty coverage. For inspection or repair, please contact an authorized service center.,['Manual15_4', 'Manual15_5', 'Manual15_6', 'Manual15_7', 'Manual15_8']
