To ensure safe operation during pressure cooking, please pay close attention to each stage of the process and follow the precautions below.

1. Pre-heating stage

<PIC>

During pre-heating, the cooker heats the liquid in the inner pot and turns it into steam. As steam builds up, pressure increases inside the cooker. Once sufficient pressure is reached, the float valve rises and locks the lid in place.

For safe operation during this stage:
- Make sure there is enough liquid in the inner pot before starting pressure cooking.
- Do not try to open the lid once the float valve has risen.
- Keep your hands and face away from the steam release area.
- Allow extra time for frozen foods, as they usually take longer to pre-heat and pressurize.

2. Cooking stage

<PIC>

Once the required pressure level is reached, the cooker begins the cooking countdown. During this stage, the cooker automatically maintains the selected pressure level, either High or Low.

For safe operation during cooking:
- Do not move, shake, or force open the cooker while it is pressurized.
- Do not cover or block the steam release valve.
- Keep children and untrained users away from the cooker while it is operating.
- Remember that higher pressure means a higher cooking temperature, so the cooker and steam can become very hot.

3. Depressurization stage

<PIC>

After cooking is complete, the food may be finished, but the cooker can still be pressurized and hot. The pressure must be safely reduced before the lid can be removed. Follow the recipe’s depressurization method and use the appropriate venting technique.

Important safety warnings:

DANGER: Do not attempt to remove the lid while the float valve is up. Never force the lid open. The contents are under extreme pressure, and the float valve must be fully down before you try to remove the lid. Ignoring this can cause serious personal injury or property damage.

WARNING: Steam released from the steam release valve is very hot. Do not cover the steam release valve, and do not place your hands, face, or exposed skin over the valve while releasing pressure.

NOTICE: Do not use Quick Release when cooking fatty, oily, thick, or high-starch foods, such as stews, chilis, pasta, or congee. Also avoid Quick Release for foods that expand during cooking, such as beans and grains, as they may foam or splatter through the steam release valve.

In short, always wait until pressure has been fully released, confirm that the float valve has dropped, and keep clear of hot steam before opening the cooker.,['Manual30_6', 'Manual30_7', 'Manual30_8']

Your power toothbrush offers 3 intensity settings, so you can choose the brushing strength that feels best for your teeth and gums:

- High intensity: three lights
- Medium intensity: two lights
- Low intensity: one light

The illuminated intensity indicator lights on the handle show your current setting.

<PIC>

To adjust the intensity manually, press the intensity indicator area on the handle. Each press cycles through the available levels, allowing you to move between low, medium, and high intensity until you reach your preferred setting.

<PIC>

You can change the intensity before you start brushing, while brushing, or after brushing. If your teeth or gums feel sensitive, try starting with low intensity and increase gradually as comfortable. For a stronger clean, you can select medium or high intensity.

You can also customize the intensity setting through the app, along with other toothbrush settings.,['toothbrush0_06', 'toothbrush0_07']

You can customize your toothbrush by activating or deactivating these features:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Pressure Sensor Feedback
- Scrubbing Feedback
- Brush head replacement reminder

Please note: if Pressure Sensor Feedback is deactivated, Adaptive Intensity will also be deactivated.

You can adjust some features in the app, and others directly from the toothbrush handle.

**Using the app**

The following features can be activated or deactivated from the app:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Scrubbing Feedback

To use the app, download it on your phone, turn on Bluetooth, pick up the toothbrush so the lights turn on, then open the app and follow the guided steps to pair your toothbrush.

**Using the toothbrush handle**

1. Place the toothbrush handle on the charging stand.

2. Press and hold the power button. The length of time you hold the button determines which feature you are changing:

<PIC>

- **Adaptive Intensity**: hold for up to 3 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, and the SenseIQ indicator will light up for 3 seconds.

- **Pressure Sensor Feedback**: hold for up to 5 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, then 2 beeps, and the SenseIQ indicator plus the light ring will light up purple for 3 seconds.

- **Brush head replacement reminder**: hold for up to 7 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, then 2 beeps, then 3 beeps, and the brush head replacement reminder indicator will light up for 3 seconds.

<PIC>

After the indicator lights, check the battery indication to confirm whether the selected feature was turned on or off:

- If the battery indication blinks white 3 times and you hear 3 tones from low to high, the feature has been activated.
- If the battery indication blinks amber 3 times and you hear 3 tones from high to low, the feature has been deactivated.

So, in short: place the handle on the charger, hold the power button for the feature you want to change, then use the battery light color and tones to confirm whether the feature is on or off.,['Manual37_13', 'toothbrush0_11']

To charge the electric toothbrush while it is inside the travel case, please follow these steps:

1. Connect the USB cord to the USB-C socket on the travel case, then connect the other end to the USB wall adapter.

<PIC>

2. Plug the USB wall adapter into an electrical outlet.

3. Place the toothbrush handle into the travel case. Make sure it sits correctly in its charging position inside the case.

<PIC>

4. Check that charging has started properly. When the toothbrush is positioned correctly and power is connected, the handle will beep twice and the lights will illuminate in an upward motion. During charging, the battery indicator blinks white.

<PIC>

5. Leave the travel case plugged in until the toothbrush is fully charged. When charging is complete, the battery light will stop blinking and turn off.

<PIC>

For best stability while charging, place the travel case on its side. Also make sure the toothbrush and brush head are dry before storing them in the travel case, and use only the compatible USB wall adapter, cord, and charging travel case.,['Manual37_18', 'Manual37_19', 'Manual37_20', 'Manual37_21']
